title: Review fixes: helper-drift test (P1) + hashing-input match + label + hasOwnProperty + P2 test gaps
read_first: review findings in the session review-integrity-2026-07-15
action: Apply the fix-now review findings: (P1 test-coverage) add a test proving a content-edited managed HELPER (.bee/bin/bee.mjs, not lib) reads drift:true and names it — the checkGroup(managed.helpers) branch was untested. (P2 architecture) computeRuntimeDrift hashes readFileSync(abs,'utf8') to match buildManagedVersions' input type exactly, so the recorder/reader hashers agree by construction not incidental UTF-8 round-trip. (P2 test) add version-drift-alone (legacy map + mismatched bee_version => drift:true) and corrupt-non-JSON-ledger (fail-open, exit 0, drift:false) tests. (P3 code-quality) extra-file scan uses Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call instead of `in` (prototype-chain safety). (P3 architecture) status text label [version drift] -> [drift: N file(s)] since drift is now content/file-set drift. Strengthen the content-edit assertion to exact-path match. Mirror bee.mjs byte-identical.

artifacts: bee.mjs computeRuntimeDrift utf8-hash + hasOwnProperty + label; 3 new drift tests + strengthened assertion
prohibitions: do not weaken any existing drift assertion
do not let the mirror drift
